task_completion.description: Navigate to the System Administration area
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenMRS 'System Administration' page or dashboard is visible in the web browser.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Encounter Roles management page
task_completion.visual_evidence: A page titled 'Manage Encounter Roles' (or similar) is visible, showing a list of existing roles or an option to add a new one.
The text 'Medical Scribe' is typed into the 'Name' input field on the Add/Edit Encounter Role form.
The text 'Assists with documentation' is typed into the 'Description' input field on the Add/Edit Encounter Role form.

task_completion.description: Save the new Encounter Role and verify its creation
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message is displayed, or 'Medical Scribe' is visible as a saved entry in the list of Encounter Roles.

integrity.description: Agent used the web browser GUI to complete the task
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacts with the OpenMRS web interface in a browser, rather than using terminal commands, curl, or API scripts.

integrity.description: Agent did not use direct database manipulation
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows open showing SQL commands (e.g., INSERT INTO encounter_role) being executed to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: Results are shown within the actual application
integrity.visual_evidence: The final verification of the created role occurs within the OpenMRS application interface, not in a text editor or a locally modified HTML file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating an Encounter Role in OpenMRS. The expected name is exactly 'Medical Scribe' and the expected description is exactly 'Assists with documentation'. The agent must complete this via the System Administration UI.
